mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically boosting equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows or positive forward returns occur in less than 55% of rate-cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 8.0%, it signals severe labor market deterioration and collapsing consumer spending power, which forces equity markets to price in prolonged earnings declines and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in over 60% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 8.0%, or the signal loses predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 in post-2009 data.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized recovery in consumer demand and construction activity, which amplifies corporate revenue growth expectations and drives positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series, with interaction term coefficients statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-crisis recovery regime, month-over-month increases in the industrial production index reflect rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive 5-day equity returns.
failure_condition: Signal direction reverses during expansionary regimes, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical significance with p-values exceeding 0.10 when INDPRO rises.

mechanism_narrative: Rising nonfarm payrolls directly increase aggregate wage income and consumer purchasing power, which supports corporate earnings growth and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Positive payroll growth correlates with negative equity returns in over 50% of observations, or Rank IC falls below 0.015 across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading exceeding 0.5% triggers expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and forces negative forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in inflation pass-through successfully, yielding positive returns in over 65% of high-inflation months, invalidating the tightening fear mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of expanding real GDP and declining policy rates creates a favorable growth-and-liquidity environment that maximizes equity risk premium compression and earnings expansion, driving positive quarterly returns.
failure_condition: The interaction effect vanishes when GDP growth exceeds 3% annually, or the joint signal produces negative returns in over 55% of triggered quarters.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production falls for two consecutive months, it indicates a breakdown in supply chain coordination and falling factory orders, which forces equity markets to downgrade near-term earnings forecasts and produces negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Consecutive production declines correlate with positive equity returns in over 60% of cases, or the signal fails to predict downturns with a precision above 50%.
